_meta.doc: Hand-authored room templates (set-pieces) stamped into generated dungeon floors — generator roadmap #5. The runtime generator (src/systems/mapgen.js) carries a built-in copy (DEFAULT_PREFABS); this file is the editable override passed in as opts.prefabs (loaded by main.js, like biomes/acts).

legend.#: wall (forced solid; finalizeWalls renders it + a north face)
legend.A: floor anchor (boss/center) — plain floor, the orchestration drops the Alpha here
legend.R: relic cache event (rare — use sparingly; relic pacing is normally node-driven)

_meta.tags: ['body'] = stamped into a normal room; ['arena'] = stamped around the Alpha on boss floors (keep the center 'A' clear). A prefab only stamps into a room it fits (width <= room width, rows <= room height). Up to 2 body prefabs per floor. Reachability is re-guaranteed after stamping, so a template can never seal off the floor.
_meta.note: If you ADD prefabs here they take over from the built-in set entirely (the engine uses opts.prefabs when non-empty). Keep at least the boss_arena entry if you want a boss set-piece.
